componentDescription: Tags (also called badges or chips) display categorization labels, status indicators, and filterable attributes. Use Tags to show status (Active, Pending), categories (Design, Engineering), or removable filter chips. For filter chip groups with removal, provide onClose.
features: Multiple semantic variants for color-coded meaning

usageExamples.title: Status badge in a table
usageExamples.description: Colored tags showing entity status in a data table cell.

usageExamples.title: Removable filter chips
usageExamples.description: Active filter tags that can be removed individually.
usageExamples.code: {activeFilters.map(filter => ( removeFilter(filter.id)} variant="primary" size="md" /> ))}

usageExamples.title: Category tags with icons
usageExamples.description: Tags with icons for technology categories on a project card.
usageExamples.code: {project.technologies.map(tech => ( } variant="neutral" size="sm" /> ))}

label.llmContext: The text displayed inside the tag. Keep it very short — ideally 1-2 words. Use Title Case for status labels (Active, Pending) and lowercase for category tags.

variant.llmContext: Controls the color scheme. Use semantic variants: 'success' (green) for active/completed states, 'warning' (yellow) for pending/attention states, 'error' (red) for failed/blocked states, 'neutral' (grey) for inactive/archived, 'primary' (blue) for information/selected. Use color variants consistently so users learn the color meanings.

onClose.llmContext: Provide a callback to show a close/remove icon on the tag. Use when the tag represents an active filter, selected item, or any removable entity. Clicking the X calls this callback — remove the tag from your state in this handler.
onClose.propDescription: Callback fired when the close button is clicked; presence adds close button

size.llmContext: Use 'sm' in table cells and compact lists. Use 'md' for standard inline tags and filter chips.

icon.llmContext: React node for an icon displayed inside the tag before the label. Use for technology logos, status icons, or category icons that reinforce the tag's meaning visually.

disabled.llmContext: Set to true to show the tag in a disabled/muted state. Use when the tag represents an unavailable option or when the user cannot interact with it.
